page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The instance is configured with 58.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 948.0 and stress-ng single-core CPU performance, fall into the lower tier. However, the 64-vCPU configuration scales effectively in multi-threaded tasks, achieving an average multi-core Geekbench score of 9980.0 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 47081.36. Memory bandwidth remains stable, with read-only large block transfers reaching 77.81 GB/sec and write operations at 32.34 GB/sec.

Due to its high core count and low memory density, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads that do not demand large memory footprints. It is well-suited for tasks such as multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and batch processing. However, it is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded applications due to its weaker single-core performance and low memory-per-core allocation.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. Equipped with 58.0 GB of memory, it has a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.9 GB per vCPU and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core scaling, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 9980.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 47081.36. This resource distribution makes the instance a qualitatively cost-effective option for highly parallelized, compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, such as multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and batch processing pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 58 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance for parallelized, compute-bound workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-64 is an Intel Xeon instance with 64 vCPUs and 58 GB RAM, optimized for parallel, compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-core virtual machine designed specifically for parallelized, compute-bound workloads that require substantial multi-threaded processing power without massive memory overhead.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor
58.0 GB of system memory providing 0.9 GB of RAM per vCPU
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong multi-core scaling for parallelized workloads
Suited for multi-threaded software compilation and ray tracing
